I shared a new trade 5 weeks ago buying these $MU options when the market sold off on the ridiculous $GOOGL TurboQuant headlines (those same options are now up more than 300%).

Sometimes the market hands you a layup when FUD ignores the bigger picture that better compression may reduce memory per task but if it makes AI cheaper & drives more usage then total memory demand can still go higher.

TOP 10 AI REVENUE WINNERS

AI revenue is concentrating around the companies that either build the compute or control the cloud demand layer: 

AI Infrastructure are where AI demand gets packaged & monetized
• $CRWV could add ~$51B as dedicated AI cloud demand ramps
• $AMZN, $MSFT and $GOOGL could add ~$800B combined in AI-exposed revenue by 2029

AI Chips are where AI demand gets physically built & scaled
• $AMD could add ~$67B as its AI accelerator business scales
• $NVDA remains the clear leader with ~$450B of incremental AI-exposed revenue by 2029
• $QCOM could add ~$12B as its AI chip business expands beyond handsets into edge compute
• $AVGO & $MRVL represent ~$220B & ~$40B of incremental AI-exposed revenue by 2029 through custom silicon + networking upside

This is what happens when $MSFT, $GOOGL, $META & $AMZN all say “we are compute constrained”: 

• $NBIS +12%
• $IREN +11%
• $CRWV +7%
• $CIFR +6%
• $APLD +4%
• $DOCN +4%
• $WULF +3%

$GME CEO Ryan Cohen says GameStop could fund an $EBAY bid with half cash, half stock and “issue stock in order to get the deal done.”

Michael Burry says move “makes perfect sense” despite GameStop being worth ~$12B and eBay ~4x larger at ~$46B.

I’m literally left speechless after listening to Ryan Cohen explain how he plans to bridge that math in this interview.

$IREN up more than 10% after energizing its 1.4 GW Sweetwater 1 data center site in Texas & connecting it to the ERCOT grid.

Power ramps as new data centers come online turning Sweetwater turns into another large-scale AI campus where secured power capacity is the real asset.

Cerebras $CBRS filed updated IPO terms offering 28M shares at $115-$125 to raise ~$3B at ~$26B valuation.

The $NVDA rival grew 2025 sales 76% to $510M, turned GAAP profitable, has $20B+ OpenAI deal for 750 MW of compute & says $AMZN AWS plans to be first hyperscaler customer.
$NBIS JUST BROKE ABOVE $170 FOR THE FIRST TIME EVER

$SOFI may be preparing a consumer-facing AI product with a SoFi Principal Product Manager listing work on a “to-be-announced consumer-facing AI product.”

This could turn SoFi into a more personalized financial assistant across banking, lending, investing, credit & insurance.

Tom Lee's $BMNR now holds ~4.29% of all Ethereum.

They added ~102K $ETH last week bringing their total to ~5.18M.

$GOOGL TPUs reportedly still can’t be used in some classified contexts even as the U.S. government looks to reduce reliance on Anthropic’s Claude.

Officials are now seeking ways to accelerate clearance for Google TPUs so Gemini can be used across more classified AI workloads.

$OUST launched its Rev8 lidar lineup, including the OS1 Max which it says is the first lidar with native color sensing.

The sensor can process 10.4M points per second, detect low-reflectivity objects up to 200 meters and reach a 500-meter max range.

$AMZN is opening its full logistics network to businesses of all sizes including freight, distribution, fulfillment & parcel shipping.

This is Amazon turning its supply chain into a platform giving outside companies access to same infrastructure behind its own delivery machine.

The 30-year yield is now 8 bps away from a new 18-year high.

Both sides are looking for a way out of the current deadlock. 

Iran appears to be putting forward political proposals with limited chances of acceptance, while the United States is focusing on more practical, operational solutions, particularly around the bottleneck in the Strait of Hormuz.

Recent developments suggest that neither side believes the status quo is sustainable or working in their favor. 

As long as diplomatic efforts fail to produce meaningful progress, the ongoing friction, especially in the maritime domain, carries a real risk of escalation.

In other words, if the current situation does not lead to some form of negotiated arrangement, it is increasingly likely to result in military escalation over time.

#iran

📺 THIS IS THE BEST $PLTR SETUP WE’VE SEEN IN MONTHS + CRYPTO IS LOOKING READY

$PLTR is no longer the overhyped, “priced for perfection” trade it once was. 

After months of consolidation and a series of lower highs, the structure has reset, and risk/reward in $PLTR looks more balanced, making this one of the cleaner setups in a while. 

Still, earnings remain tricky—I don't take shares (only options), and options are expensive and even being right directionally can fail without the right structure.

Rather than forcing a trade, my focus is on waiting for confirmation.

*

At the same time, #Bitcoin and crypto-related names like $CRCL are showing stronger momentum, reclaiming key moving averages and offering more immediate opportunity.

I'm bullish on $IBIT, expecting a move toward the 200-day moving average. 

I'm also in $CRCL, which had a strong recent price action, benefiting from crypto momentum. It reclaimed key moving averages, showing technical strength.

*

If you found this helpful, please ❤️like and 🔁retweet

📺 THIS IS NOT A MARKET TOP + THE SETUPS I’M BUYING THIS WEEK

Headlines (like rumors of a missile hitting a U.S. ship) are short-lived and mostly noise, not trend-changing events.

They offer frequent intraday swings and elevated volatility, which are good trading conditions for active traders like us.

$SPY is only slightly down and already off lows. 

Some traders are calling a “topping tail” (potential reversal signal), but it’s just a wide-range consolidation, not a confirmed top.

So, my strategy here: manage ranges, use hedges tactically, and rotate exposure rather than going all-in/out.

*

Some of the stocks I'm planning to buy this week:

– $GOOGL is a strong leader. Clean breakout above February highs. Suggested long level ~$365, with upside potential toward $400. It is acting well even in a weak tape. This is your “stay long and let it work” name.

– $AMZN I currently don't have a position since it has a messier structure than Google. Possible buy zone: $262–$273 range. It needs more time to develop, but I will be open to it. 

– $AAPL had a big prior move → now consolidating. Key level is $278. Ideal scenario: dip below → reclaim → move sideways. I also don't believe it’s a double top. This is healthy digestion, not breakdown.

– $TSLA recently triggered a Red Dog reversal (washout below $368 → bounce). It is choppy, with no strong momentum. My strategy: gradually accumulate on dips, avoid chasing strength. It is now slightly pulling back after a 2-day push. This is a trader’s stock right now, not a clean trend. 

*

So, this week, don’t chase momentum (it’s inconsistent), only buy against levels (define risk tightly), scale in/out instead of full-size entries, use hedges actively, focus on leaders (like $GOOGL), and avoid forcing trades in weaker setups.

The market is not breaking down, but it’s also not cleanly trending. News-driven volatility continues to create opportunities for active traders, so use them. 

Leadership exists, but many names require precision and patience. 

*

If you found this helpful, please ❤️like and 🔁retweet
